channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Iran announces 'end of military operations' against Israel, but warns Lebanon strikes could trigger escalation
Nasdaq futures gain 1% as chip stocks rebound from rout, Trump tries to maintain ceasefire: Live updates
item.description: Investors will also be focused on inflation data and the public debut of SpaceX on Friday.

item.title: USAA to return nearly $1 billion to Florida members as legal reforms help lower insurance costs
item.description: USAA said it will deliver nearly $1 billion in combined savings and returns to eligible Florida members, including a $500 million dividend, CNBC can exclusively report.

item.title: Oil prices pull back from highs after Trump says Israel and Iran want a ceasefire
item.description: Oil rises on Monday amid heightened tensions in the Middle East, raising concerns that the conflict may drag on.

item.title: Bank of America hikes Sandisk price target to $2,100. Here's why
item.description: The bank thinks the memory giant has more room to run thanks to agreements with customers and lingering shortages due to the AI buildout.

item.title: Weight loss drug maker sinks 25% after new safety data spooks investors
item.description: While the experimental drug met its key targets, analysts are concerned over the commercial opportunity due to side effects.

item.title: Marvell Technology jumps almost 9% in premarket after news it will join the S&P 500 index
item.description: Marvell rose in premarket trading after S&P Global said the AI chipmaker would join the S&P 500 on June 22.

item.title: Trump storms out of interview after being challenged about election fraud claims, DOJ fund
item.description: The president said he would like to see the weaponization fund proceed despite setbacks.

item.title: 'Bring 'em on': Delta wants United's crown over the Pacific, too
item.description: Delta's president, Peter Carter, told CNBC that the carrier wants to take United on over the Pacific.

item.title: China is helping to cushion global oil prices below $100 — but analysts warn it won’t last
item.description: China has reduced oil imports since the start of the Iran war, capping global crude prices.

item.title: Airline profits set to halve this year as fuel costs jump by $100 billion: IATA
item.description: "The big unknown is how long travelers and shippers can tolerate the higher costs," IATA's Willie Walsh said.

item.title: Women often outlive men. Here's how they should approach their long-term care needs
item.description: In addition to typically living longer than their husbands, women also are more likely to need long-term care, which can be costly.

item.title: Bidding war erupts for world’s oldest bank as Italy’s Intesa gatecrashes BPM offer
item.description: Intesa on Monday announced an unsolicited offer of $35.3 billion, hoping to usurp its rival and create Europe's second-biggest ​bank by market capitalization.

item.title: China's Xi to visit North Korea for first time in seven years as Beijing tests its influence over Kim
item.description: North Korea may use the summit as an opportunity to press for economic concessions or Beijing's "tacit recognition" of its nuclear status, analysts say.

item.title: Earthquake of magnitude 7.8 strikes off southern Philippines, 32 feared dead
item.description: The death toll in a powerful 7.8‑magnitude earthquake off the southern Philippine island of Mindanao on Monday has risen to at least 32.

item.title: Inside the Obama-era Iran nuclear deal that Trump withdrew from
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have yet to reach a peace deal or address Iran's nuclear ambitions, despite signals from Trump that talks are progressing.

item.title: Foreign investors have dumped billions of dollars of Korean stocks this year despite record rally. Here's why
item.description: The selling intensified Monday as the benchmark Kospi plunged more than 8% at the open, even as it emerged as one of the world's standout performers thus far.

item.title: 'Dean of Valuation' Aswath Damodaran is not buying SpaceX: 'Too richly priced'
item.description: The NYU professor said he understands why some people are getting ready to pay up for SpaceX, but he is "more investor than trader."

item.title: U.S. confirms second Texas screwworm case, Canada restricts livestock imports
item.description: New World screwworm larvae feed on the living tissue of warm-blooded animals, creating severe wounds that can be fatal if left untreated.

item.title: Why AI hyperscalers are now the epicenter of a bear case for stocks
item.description: In the near term, there are many mouths to feed and not enough to eat.

item.title: Remote work career risks for Gen Z are 'subtle, but material,' Harvard professor says: How to avoid the pitfalls
item.description: Forgoing the office can have detrimental "scarring" impacts on young workers. Here's how to navigate the benefits and drawbacks of remote work.

item.title: Is a reverse mortgage right for me?
item.description: Reverse mortgages can help people tap their home equity without a monthly payment, but they're risky.

item.title: Harry's and Coterie owner Mammoth Brands has ambitions to be the next CPG giant
item.description: Mammoth's direct-to-consumer brands have helped upend the razor, diaper and deodorant categories.

item.title: 100 days of the Iran war: How global markets and the economy have been affected, in charts
item.description: The conflict is about to reach its 100th day, with peace negotiations hanging in the balance.

item.title: Why Nokia missed the smartphone shift
item.description: For years, Nokia didn't just compete in mobile phones — it defined the category. From the iconic 3310 to the best-selling Nokia 1100, the Finnish company built a global empire on durable devices, mass-market appeal and an unmatched ability to scale. At its peak, Nokia was Europe's most valuable company and one of the most dominant consumer tech brands in the world. But the smartphone era changed where value in the industry was created.

item.title: How ‘Backrooms’ producer Peter Chernin thinks Hollywood needs to change
item.description: Franchise fatigue could drive an entertainment industry sea change as the appetite for fresh ideas grows and younger audiences make up more box office sales.

item.title: Tech stock sell-off accelerates; SoftBank drops 6% as investors sour on AI-linked names
item.description: Global tech stocks tracked overnight losses in U.S. tech names led by a sell-off in Broadcom.

item.title: Inflation inside the electronics you buy may soon become a bit more sticky
item.description: Most consumers are not familiar with the role resin plays inside electronics like smartphones. A current shortage of the materials may push up prices.

item.title: Bitcoin is cratering, but a new Wall Street crypto hype is on the rise
item.description: As bitcoin dropped to its lowest price since 2024, investors flock to a new type of crypto investment linked to the hyperliquid platforms, HYPE ETFs.

item.title: No bags, no watch parties at Madison Square Garden with Trump attending Game 3 of the NBA Finals
item.description: Trump is a longtime Knicks fan who confirmed Friday that he would attend the first NBA Finals game in New York since 1999.
